title: Set up torch-npu virtual environment with CPU-base torch and +cpu variant torchvision
symptom: pip install torch-npu resolves to incompatible torch version (e.g., 2.11.0) instead of matching system torch-npu (2.5.1). torchvision fails with 'RuntimeError: operator torchvision::nms does not exist' due to dispatch key mismatch with CPU-only torch.
root_cause: The torch-npu package declares a strict dependency on the CPU-only build of PyTorch (e.g., torch==2.5.1+cpu). pip's resolver initially pulls the standard CUDA build of torch from generic PyPI mirrors, causing a version conflict. Additionally, vanilla torchvision expects CUDA-enabled torch and registers operators with the 'CUDA' dispatch key, which conflicts with the CPU-base torch's 'CPU' dispatch key.
fix_steps: 1. Probe the system-wide torch-npu version installed by CANN: python -c 'import torch_npu; print(torch_npu.__version__)'. Note the major.minor version (e.g., 2.5.1).
2. Create a virtual environment: python -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate.

3. Install the matching CPU-base torch from PyTorch's official CPU wheel index: pip install torch==<version>+cpu --extra-index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cpu --trusted-host download.pytorch.org.
4. Install the matching torch-npu from a domestic mirror: pip install torch-npu==<version> -i https://mirrors.aliyun.com/pypi/simple/.

5. Install the matching CPU-variant torchvision: pip install torchvision==<derived_version>+cpu --extra-index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cpu --trusted-host download.pytorch.org. Derive torchvision version as: if torch is 2.5.x, torchvision is 0.20.x; if torch is 2.4.x, torchvision is 0.19.x.
6. Install pyyaml if not already present: pip install pyyaml. (torch_npu requires yaml at import time for its memory visualization module.)

7. Verify the stack: python -c 'import torch; import torch_npu; import torchvision; print(torch.__version__); print(torch_npu.__version__); print(torch.npu.is_available())'. Expected: torch shows '+cpu', torch_npu shows the probed version, npu.is_available() returns True.
affected_patterns: pip install -r requirements.txt  # where requirements.txt contains 'torch' and 'torch-npu' without version pins
pip install torch torch-npu torchvision  # resolves to incompatible versions
antipatterns: Do NOT install torch from a generic PyPI mirror first — it will pull the CUDA build and conflict with torch-npu's torch==X.X.X+cpu dependency.
Do NOT install vanilla torchvision on top of CPU-base torch — the torchvision::nms operator registration will fail with a dispatch key conflict. Always use the +cpu variant.
Do NOT skip the version probe step — system torch-npu version must match the torch version exactly (e.g., torch-npu 2.5.1 requires torch 2.5.1+cpu, not 2.6.1+cpu).

meta.refiner_reasoning: This experience was confirmed on an Ascend 910B2C host with CANN 8.1.RC1 and Python 3.10.17. Phase 2 venv creation took 280 seconds due to pip version backtracking across 20+ torch-npu versions before settling on the compatible stack. The fix (installing torch==2.5.1+cpu from PyTorch CPU index, then torch-npu==2.5.1, then torchvision==0.20.1+cpu) resolved all conflicts and verified with torch.npu.is_available()==True and successful execution of train.py (exit_code=0, 12.14s training on NPU). This pattern applies universally to any Ascend environment using torch-npu in a clean venv.

problem_description: When creating a clean virtual environment for torch-npu, `pip install torch-npu` resolves `torch` to the latest version (e.g., 2.11.0) which is incompatible with the system torch-npu (2.5.1). Additionally, torch-npu==2.5.1 declares a strict dependency on `torch==2.5.1+cpu` (CPU-only build), and installing vanilla torchvision causes `torchvision::nms` dispatch key conflicts with the CPU-base torch. This is the most common blocker in CUDA-to-NPU migrations using venvs.
rough_fix_approach: 1. Probe system torch-npu version first (`python -c 'import torch_npu; print(torch_npu.__version__)'`). 2. Install matching CPU-base torch from PyTorch index: `pip install torch==<version>+cpu --extra-index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cpu`. 3. Install torch-npu==<version> from domestic mirror. 4. Install CPU-variant torchvision: `pip install torchvision==<version>+cpu --extra-index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cpu`. 5. Verify with `torch.npu.is_available()`.
